weekDates: September 21 – 27, 2026 · Block 4 · Week 4 of 4 · COMP WEEK (Sept 27)
weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. This week is the exception — do less, not more: 2–4 short patterns total, just enough to stay loose. Warm up gently; stop well before fatigue.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — taper into comp day (Sept 27). Deload: very light movement-prep only, nothing taxing. The training is done — this week is about arriving fresh, loose, and confident. Everything is fresh this week; keep loads easy, reps few, and stay well under 3 sets. If in doubt, do less. Rest is part of the plan.
testsLineHtml: No tests — comp week. Save it for the platform on Sept 27. Just light movement prep this week — no maxing, no working numbers to chase. Show up fresh.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 2 × 5 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · very light, short and snappy movement prep
patterns.load: A KB 1–2 sizes LIGHTER than competition, at competition box height
cues: Keep it short and easy — just remind your legs of the comp pattern.
Stand up snappy and tall, but stop while everything still feels fresh.
No grinding and no fatigue — you want to arrive primed for comp day.
Touch the box clean and stand fully on every easy rep.

patterns.name: Bodyweight Sit-to-Stand Loosener
patterns.rx: 2 × 6 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · slow easy reps to stay loose
cues: Sit to the box and stand slowly just to stay loose and mobile.
Keep effort very low — this should feel restful, not like training.
Breathe easy and relax your shoulders through each rep.
Stop immediately if anything feels tight or tired.

patterns.name: Standing Quad Activation
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 45 sec between sets · light controlled knee extensions, no load
cues: Stand tall and gently straighten and bend one knee to wake up the quad.
Keep it light and snappy with no strain at all.
Hold a wall or rack for balance if you need it.
This is just to stay primed — finish completely fresh for comp day.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 2 throws · rest 90 sec between sets · 4 easy snappy throws, very light, just stay loose
cues: Take a few easy, snappy throws up and back overhead — just enough to feel fast and loose for comp day.
Keep these very light and short; this is taper week, so prime the pattern without any fatigue.
Quick hip snap, relaxed arms — stop while the throws still feel crisp and effortless.
Arrive at comp day Sept 27 fresh — never chase distance this week.

patterns.name: Walking Hip Openers
patterns.rx: 1 × 6 reps each side · easy mobility only
cues: Slowly lift one knee up and open it out to the side, then step down — loosen the hips with easy, controlled circles.
Move at a relaxed walking pace and keep every circle smooth, never forcing the range or rushing the reps.
Hold a wall or rail for balance if you need it and keep every rep smooth.
Stop the moment you feel warm and loose; freshness is the goal.

patterns.name: Arm Circles — Easy
patterns.rx: 1 × 10 reps each direction · light shake-out only
cues: Make slow, easy circles with both arms, forward then backward, gradually opening up your shoulders.
Keep it light and short — just warming the shoulders so you feel loose and ready on comp day.
Stay relaxed and never force the range; this should feel like a gentle shake-out.
Finish feeling primed and fresh, not worked.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 1 light set · ~30% of your max-rep test at comp weight · just enough to feel sharp
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — do one easy, snappy set to stay loose for comp day, nothing more.
Every rep should feel fast and effortless. This is movement prep, not training — leave loads in the tank.
Stop the moment it feels like work. Arrive fresh on Sept 27.

patterns.name: Empty-Bar Press
patterns.rx: 1 × 8 reps · slow, easy, full range
patterns.load: An empty barbell or two very light DBs
cues: Press an empty bar or very light DBs for a few smooth reps just to grease the movement.
Keep it slow and easy through a full range — this is a loosener, not a workout.
Stop after one easy set. The only goal is to feel primed and fresh.

patterns.name: Arm Circles
patterns.rx: 1 × 20 sec each direction · easy and loose
cues: Stretch both arms out to the sides and make small, easy circles forward, then backward, to warm the shoulders.
Keep it gentle and loose — this is just movement prep to stay snappy for comp day.
No strain, no fatigue. Stop while it still feels easy.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 10-second easy hold · rest as needed · movement prep only
patterns.load: Pull-up bar — very light, easy 10-second holds just to feel the bar, nothing taxing
cues: Hop up, feel a packed-shoulder hang for 10 seconds, and step down fresh.
Keep it short and snappy — this is loosening up, not training.
No grinding and no near-failure holds; arrive primed for comp day Sept 27.
Crush the bar lightly so the pattern feels familiar, then rest.

patterns.name: Arm Swing Mobility
patterns.rx: 1 × 10 easy swings each direction
patterns.load: Bodyweight — gentle shoulder circles and arm swings, no load
cues: Swing the arms forward and back, then big circles, to loosen the shoulders.
Keep everything light, smooth, and pain-free.
This is movement prep to feel loose, nothing more.
Stop before any fatigue — full freshness is the goal.

patterns.name: Easy Band Pull-Apart
patterns.rx: 1 × 12 reps
patterns.load: A light band held at chest height — easy tension only
cues: Hold the band out front and pull your hands apart, squeezing the shoulder blades.
Keep it light and snappy to wake up the upper back.
No straining — this is a primer, not a workout.
Finish feeling loose and ready, not worked.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 2 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between · easy and snappy
patterns.load: A light D-ball, 6 lb (women) / 12 lb (men) — well under your comp D-ball for the deload
cues: Just a few easy, snappy slams with the light ball to stay loose — nothing taxing this week.
Full reach and a clean slam, but stop while it still feels effortless.
Arrive at comp fresh; this is movement-prep only.

patterns.name: D-Ball Halo
patterns.rx: 2 × 5 reps each direction · rest 60 sec between sets
cues: Circle the light ball slowly around your head, keeping your trunk tall and braced.
Move smooth and easy — this is gentle shoulder and overhead prep, not a workout.
Switch directions each round and keep it relaxed.

patterns.name: Band Shoulder Pass-Through
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest 45 sec between sets · slow and easy
patterns.load: A light resistance band held wide in both hands
cues: Hold a light band wide and sweep it slowly from in front of your thighs up over your head to behind you, then reverse.
Keep your arms straight and only travel as far as your shoulders comfortably allow.
Easy taper-week mobility — no force, just open the shoulders before comp week.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 1 × 30 sec of easy singles · just one short, snappy set · stop fresh
patterns.load: Your jump rope, very easy movement-prep pace
cues: Just one short, easy set to stay loose — nothing taxing this week.
Keep it light and snappy and finish feeling completely fresh.
Save every bit of energy for comp day on Sept 27.

patterns.name: Gentle Calf Wake-Up Raise
patterns.rx: 1 × 12 reps · bodyweight · slow and easy
cues: Do one easy set of slow rises just to keep the ankles moving.
No weight and no burn — this is movement prep only.
Stop the moment it feels like work.

patterns.name: Loose Ankle Bounces
patterns.rx: 1 × 20 sec of soft bounces · light and relaxed
cues: Bounce softly on the balls of your feet to stay springy and relaxed.
Keep it tiny and easy — you are just staying loose for comp day.
Stop while you feel fresh and bouncy, never tired.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 2 jumps · rest 90 sec between · very light, snappy rehearsal
cues: Take just a couple of easy, snappy practice jumps to feel the competition motion — well under any real effort.
Swing the arms, dip, jump forward at a relaxed pace, and stick both feet cleanly.
Reset between reps and keep it short. Save every bit of spring for comp day.

patterns.name: Easy Skip in Place
patterns.rx: 2 × 20 sec · rest 60 sec between · light and loose
cues: Skip gently in place, driving each knee up easily and staying light on the balls of your feet.
Keep it short and snappy just to wake the legs up — nothing taxing this close to comp day.
Stop the moment you feel loose and warm. This is movement prep, not a workout.

patterns.name: Soft Two-Foot Hop-and-Stick
patterns.rx: 2 × 3 hops · rest 60 sec between · tiny hops, perfect landings
cues: Take a small, easy two-foot hop forward and stick the landing softly — just grooving the pattern.
Keep the hops tiny and the effort low; the goal is to stay primed and fresh, not to build anything.
Reset between reps and stop early. Arrive at comp day loose and rested.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 1 × 200m easy-snappy · one short piece only · well under all-out
patterns.load: Rower · light and quick — just a few sharp strokes to stay primed, not a workout
cues: One short, snappy 200m to stay loose — feel fast, but keep it easy.
A couple of crisp strokes at pace, then back off — nothing taxing this week.
Get off the rower fresh; the goal is freshness for comp day, not fitness.

patterns.name: Standing Row Posture Drill
patterns.rx: 1 × 8 easy reps · light, movement-prep only
patterns.load: A very light band — barely any tension, just grooving the pattern
cues: Pull a light band to your ribs and stand tall to rehearse a clean finish position.
Keep it feather-light — this is movement prep, not strengthening.
Move slow and easy, then you're done; stay loose and rested for comp day.

patterns.name: Gentle Cat-Cow Flow
patterns.rx: 1 × 8 slow reps · easy mobility only
patterns.load: Bodyweight on hands and knees · no load, just easy movement
cues: On hands and knees, slowly round your back up, then let it sag and lift your chest.
Move with your breath and keep it gentle — this loosens the spine, nothing more.
No straining at the ends — just a few easy reps to feel loose for comp day.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 50 yd · easy stride into one smooth turn · rest as needed
patterns.load: Bodyweight · very light, snappy movement prep only — nowhere near a hard effort
cues: Stride one easy 50, make one smooth controlled turn, and stride back — just to feel the pattern.
Stay relaxed and loose; this is movement prep to arrive primed, not a workout.
Two short reps and you're done — freshness for comp day is the whole goal.

patterns.name: Walking Leg Swings
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 swings per leg · gentle and controlled · no rest needed
cues: Swing each leg gently forward and back through a comfortable range, holding a wall for balance.
Keep the swings smooth and easy — this loosens the hips without any strain.
Stay light and brief; nothing taxing this close to comp.

patterns.name: Quick Feet Taps
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 seconds of fast foot taps · light and snappy · rest 60 sec
cues: Tap your feet quickly in place on the balls of your feet, staying light and relaxed.
Keep it brief and snappy to wake up your turnover — barely any effort.
Two short bursts is plenty; arrive fresh and ready for comp day.

events.tag: Peak + Taper · comp-week taper (sled, not tested)

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 1 × 30 yards · easy and snappy · movement prep only (not timed, never a test)
patterns.load: Light: drop to 1 × 25 lb plate per side (women) / 1 × 45 lb plate per side (men) — well under comp weight
cues: Just one easy, light 30-yard push to feel the movement — stay snappy and well under comp weight, and never make this a timed test.
Run through your setup: 45° angle, arms locked, push through the trunk — a quick rehearsal, not a workout.
Stop completely fresh; the only goal this week is arriving loose and primed for comp day.

patterns.name: Bodyweight Reverse Lunge to Reach
patterns.rx: 1 × 6 reps per leg · easy pace · rest as needed
cues: Step back into a shallow lunge and reach your arms up to gently open your hips and stay loose for comp day.
Move slow and tall with no load — this is light movement prep, nothing taxing.
Do just enough to feel warm and springy, then stop.

patterns.name: Ankle Bounce
patterns.rx: 1 × 12 reps · easy, quiet bounces · rest as needed
patterns.load: Bodyweight only, small bounces off the balls of your feet
cues: Do small, quiet bounces on the balls of your feet to prime your legs and ankles for race day.
Keep your legs almost straight and the bounces tiny and springy — no deep knee bend.
Keep it short and snappy and stop while you feel fresh; freshness is the whole point this week.
